html:
Pine Cones can be planted to grow into Evergreen trees. It takes from 7.5 to 10 game hours for them to grow from saplings into small trees. They can also be used as a low-level fuel item. Planted saplings will not appear on the Map until they have progressed into the small tree stage.

Pine Cones can't be planted on Checkerboard Floorings, Wooden Floorings, Cobblestones, Carpeted Floorings, Rocky Turfs and Cave Rock Turfs, unless it has been planted before these turfs were placed.

When chopping down trees, it should be noted that chopping down a Large Evergreen will provide two Pine Cones, a Medium Evergreen will provide one, and a Small Evergreen will provide none.

Planting Pine Cones can calm and put to sleep nearby Treeguards, whether or not they are aggressive. The chance of this happening is a maximum of 33% when planted near the Treeguard, with a lower chance when planted farther away. The Treeguard will groan in acknowledgement when a Pine Cone is planted within range, even if it has already been pacified.

Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, Pine Cones can be bought for 1 Oinc at the Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements in the Pig City; as they are cheap, they are recommended for planting a pine tree farm as soon as possible, as Rainforest Trees only drop seeds during Lush Season, and Tea Trees drop less logs.

Trivia

  • In older versions of the game, when the player planted a cone, the plant first sprouted as a small tree, rather than a new sapling.

Gallery

  • Prompt that appears when a Pine Cone is held over a plantable spot.
  • An Evergreen sapling produced by planting a Pine Cone.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.

–Willow

–Woodie

“My favorite portable Fibonacci sequence.”
–Wagstaff

“Not very good for eating, florp.”
–Wurt
“I should find a good spot to plant this.”
–Walter
“The beginnings of a tree.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Quả Thông có thể được trồng để phát triển thành cây Thường Xanh. Phải mất từ ​​7,5 đến 10 giờ chơi để chúng phát triển từ cây non thành cây nhỏ. Chúng cũng có thể được sử dụng như một vật phẩm nhiên liệu cấp thấp. Cây non đã trồng sẽ không xuất hiện trên Bản đồ cho đến khi chúng chuyển sang giai đoạn cây nhỏ.

It takes from 7.5 to 10 game hours for them to grow from saplings into small trees. They can also be used as a low-level [[Fire#Fuel Values|fuel]] item. Planted saplings will not appear on the [[Map]] until they have progressed into the small tree stage. Pine Cones can't be planted on [[Checkerboard Flooring]]s, [[Wooden Flooring]]s, [[Cobblestone]]s, [[Carpeted Flooring]]s, [[Rocky Turf]]s and [[Cave Rock Turf]]s, unless it has been planted before these [[turf]]s were placed. When chopping down trees, it should be noted that chopping down a Large Evergreen will provide two Pine Cones, a Medium Evergreen will provide one, and a Small Evergreen will provide none. Planting Pine Cones can calm and put to sleep nearby [[Treeguard]]s, whether or not they are aggressive. The chance of this happening is a maximum of 33% when planted near the Treeguard, with a lower chance when planted farther away. The Treeguard will groan in acknowledgement when a Pine Cone is planted within range, even if it has already been pacified. ==[[File:Gift Icon.png|32px]] Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Pine Cones can be bought for 1 [[Oinc]] at the [[Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements]] in the [[Pig City]]; as they are cheap, they are recommended for planting a pine tree farm as soon as possible, as [[Rainforest Tree|Rainforest Trees]] only drop seeds during [[Seasons/Lush|Lush Season]], and [[Tea Tree|Tea Trees]] drop less logs. =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== *In older versions of the game, when the player planted a cone, the plant first sprouted as a small tree, rather than a new sapling. =={{pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Pine Cone prompt.png|Prompt that appears when a Pine Cone is held over a plantable spot.
